evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Ensure data products follow CI/CD best practices, adhere to data quality frameworks with assertion checks, and apply performance optimization
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The responsibilities emphasize CI/CD best practices, data quality frameworks with assertion checks, and governance, with rigour signals dominating and no velocity language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Drive commercial data availability by collaborating with digital product teams to ensure quality data collection
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: This is commercial analytics data supporting business decisions at a large global company without explicit financial-reporting or regulatory language in responsibilities (compliance appears only as a nice-to-have), placing it at moderate.
evidence.collaboration_width: Analytics Interface; Commercial Analytics; Analytics Innovation & Automation; Data Office Product
evidence.data_team_maturity: Lead a team of 5 Analytics Engineers & Associate Analytics Engineers in the Analytics Engineering Delivery sub-team
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Multiple named data sub-teams with distinct charters (Analytics Interface, Commercial Analytics, Analytics Innovation & Automation, Data Office Product) at a large global company indicate an established, mature, specialized data function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Drive Unity Catalog governance including schemas, access, and metadata tagging for improved data accessibility
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Naming a specific governance tool (Unity Catalog) with precise application context (schemas, access, metadata tagging) shows technical specificity from someone who has done this job.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Collaborate with Analytics Interface, Commercial Analytics, and business teams to convert requirements into AI-enabling data product backlog
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role's primary output feeds Commercial Analytics and commercial data availability, placing the emphasis on GTM/commercial stakeholders over other functions.
evidence.autonomy_level: Champion best practices in data product development across the organization
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role combines strategic ownership of governance, standards, and team leadership with execution work converting specific business requirements into a backlog, reflecting genuine mixed autonomy typical of a lead role.
evidence.ai_role: Collaborate with Analytics Interface, Commercial Analytics, and business teams to convert requirements into AI-enabling data product backlog
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is expected to build and lead delivery of AI-enabling data products, making them an enabler of downstream AI systems rather than a personal user of AI coding tools.
evidence.testing_framing: adhere to data quality frameworks with assertion checks
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality frameworks with assertion checks are framed as an owned responsibility the team ensures and adheres to, using an explicit ownership verb, making this a responsibility.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Ensure data products follow CI/CD best practices, adhere to data quality frameworks with assertion checks, and apply performance optimization
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Data quality frameworks, governance, and assertion checks appear as a primary named responsibility early in the list, with compliance (GDPR, Competition Law) also referenced, reflecting high concern about data trustworthiness reaching commercial decision-makers.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Drive Unity Catalog governance including schemas, access, and metadata tagging for improved data accessibility
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role governs, standardizes, and optimizes existing data products and a named platform at a mature global company rather than building from scratch, indicating fix/scale work.
evidence.work_arrangement: Hybrid work policy (average 3 days per week in office)
evidence.loss_aversion: adhere to data quality frameworks with assertion checks — reflects concern about data trustworthiness and governance reaching commercial decision-makers, reinforced by compliance (GDPR, Competition Law) references.
